Sabre Benchmark Results

This report describes benchmark tests for The Sabre Narrative Planner version 0.8 by Stephen G. Ware.

12 planners were tested on 27 problems. Each test was repeated 10 times, and the order of actions was shuffled each time, for a total of 3240 tests.

Each search was limited to visiting 1000000 nodes. There was no limit on the number of nodes a search could generate. There was no time limit on a search.

Tests were run on the Oracle Corporation Java Runtime Environment version 26, running on the Windows 11 operating system version 10.0. The JRE used 536 GB 870 MB 911 KB 1000 B of memory. It had a maximum memory of 536 GB 870 MB 911 KB 1000 B.

Tests began on May 12, 2026 at 10:08:16 EDT and ended on May 16, 2026 at 18:29:06 EDT; they took 4 days 8 hours 20 minutes 49 seconds 922 milliseconds. Tests ran on 10 threads representing a total of 43 days 10 hours 40 minutes 3 seconds 859 milliseconds of computation time.

This report was automatically generated by the The Sabre Benchmark Tool version 1.1 by Stephen G. Ware.

Problems

Information on the test problems is given in the table below. It shows the number of elements in each problem, including the number of fluent, action, and trigger templates in the original problem and the number of ground fluents, actions, and triggers created from those templates after the problem was compiled and simplified. It gives the problem goal and limits on search. Problems are ranked from easiest to hardest based on how many times they were solved, average nodes visited to solve, average nodes generated to solve, and average time spent to solve.

Name Characters Entities Fluent Templates Ground Fluents Action Templates Ground Actions Trigger Templates Ground Triggers Goal Author Temporal Limit Character Temporal Limit Epistemic Limit Times Solved Unique Planners Solved Min Nodes Visited Max Nodes Visited Avg. Planners

Information on the planners tested is given in the table below. Planners are ranked from best performing to worst performing based on number of problems solved, nodes visited, nodes generated, and time spent.
